page: Google Cloud Platform a3-highgpu-1g is an accelerator-optimized server configured with an Intel Xeon 8481C processor and a single NVIDIA H100 GPU. The CPU architecture is x86_64, featuring 26 vCPUs across 13 physical cores with dedicated allocation and a base clock speed of 2.7 GHz. The system includes 234 GB of RAM, providing 9.0 GB of memory per core. The bundled NVIDIA H100 GPU belongs to the Hopper family and features 80 GB of VRAM. Storage is provided via a local 804 GB NVMe SSD.

Benchmark results indicate strong performance in specific workloads. In single-threaded tasks, the server achieves a Geekbench single-core score of 2072.0 and a single-threaded gzip compression rate of 33.6 GB/sec, both placing in the top 25%. Text processing performance is in the top 10%, scoring 2808.0 on Geekbench. Multi-core performance is moderate, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 27427.61 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 14893.0, both falling within the average range. Large block memory write bandwidth is measured at 31.36 GB/sec.

For language model workloads, the server demonstrates a clear performance tradeoff based on model size. LLM inference text generation for a small 135M model reaches 348.05 tokens per second, placing it in the top 10% tier. However, text generation speed drops to 23.8 tokens per second for a medium 7B model, and falls to a lower-tier 2.49 tokens per second for a large 70B model. This indicates the single-GPU configuration is highly efficient for smaller models but faces resource constraints with very large models. From a resource density perspective, the single-GPU configuration provides a cost-efficient entry point for GPU-accelerated workloads, avoiding the higher resource overhead of multi-GPU nodes. The hardware profile is suited for machine learning inference of small to medium models, data processing, and GPU-accelerated compute tasks.
description: Google Cloud Platform a3-highgpu-1g is an accelerator-optimized server featuring an Intel Xeon 8481C processor with 26 dedicated vCPUs and 234 GB of RAM. It is equipped with a single NVIDIA H100 GPU from the Hopper family, providing 80 GB of VRAM, alongside an 804 GB local NVMe SSD. Benchmarks show strong single-core performance, with Geekbench single-core scores and single-threaded gzip compression rates in the top 25%, and text processing in the top 10%. For language model inference, the server delivers top-tier performance of 348.05 tokens per second on small 135M models, though performance decreases to 2.49 tokens per second for large 70B models. This server is designed for GPU-accelerated workloads, machine learning inference of small to medium models, and data processing tasks requiring dedicated CPU and high-speed local storage.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform a3-highgpu-1g is an accelerator-optimized server with 26 vCPUs, 234 GB RAM, an 804 GB NVMe SSD, and one 80 GB NVIDIA H100 GPU, designed for machine learning and GPU compute.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform a3-highgpu-1g features 26 vCPUs, 234 GB RAM, and one 80 GB NVIDIA H100 GPU for accelerated machine learning workloads.
tagline: An accelerator-optimized server featuring a dedicated Intel Xeon processor, local NVMe storage, and an NVIDIA H100 GPU with 80 GB VRAM.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 8481C processor with 26 vCPUs and 234 GB RAM
Single NVIDIA H100 Hopper GPU with 80 GB VRAM
Bundled 804 GB local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier text generation performance of 348.05 tokens/sec for small LLM models
Strong single-core performance and text processing capabilities
Suited for machine learning inference, data processing, and GPU-accelerated tasks
